title: Corner Squeeze
setup: Black's king is in the **a8** corner; your king on **c7** holds {{b7}}, {{b8}} and {{c8}}.
hint: Swing the rook to the **a-file** — the king's corner becomes his coffin.
explanation:
## The Idea

A classic **K+R vs K corner mate**. The white king is one square diagonally adjacent to the corner — the standard cage — and the rook delivers from the same file.

Play {{Ra1#}}.

Why is the king stuck?

- {{a7}} and {{a8}} — both attacked by the rook on a1 (a-file check)
- {{b7}}, {{b8}} — covered by your king on **c7**
- The rook on a1 is too far for {{Kxa1}}
- Nothing on the a-file can interpose

> **Takeaway:** Once your king reaches the *opposition diagonal* (here, c7 vs a8), the rook can deliver mate from any safe square on the file or rank where the trapped king sits.
